data.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × child 59ef "Verified fake: contract tests run against BOTH real and fake AcliClient"), cross-checked against the full sibling roster.

This child's contribution is the verified-fake honesty layer: it pins the FakeAcliClient's enrichment return shape to the real AcliClient's, so a future real-API change surfaces as fake-drift. That directly contributes to parent SC1 ("Producer (fetcher+enrichment) and consumers can no longer drift on snapshot shape without a test failing") and to parent AC1's intent (the shared shape is honest at the source), complementing 8937 (schema SSOT + round-trip contract) and fe57 (fixtures + fake). Its AC3 ("structure/type, not exact values — anti-change-detector") applies the anti-change-detector posture to its own tests. No parent criterion is left uncovered *because of this child*; the criteria it does not touch are correctly delegated to named siblings (AC2/SC1→8937; AC3/SC2→33d0; AC4/SC3→2359; AC5/SC4→822a; fixtures/fake foundation→fe57). No coverage finding against child 59ef itself.

One ROSTER-LEVEL absence (not this child's fault, reportable per G3 — already raised on sibling 33d0; restated here for this pairing's record):
- finding: Parent AC6 ("A documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNS new tests") is only PARTIALLY covered — applied per-test, not owned as a documented governing artifact.
  - criteria: parent AC6
  - location: parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 Acceptance Criteria bullet 6; cross-checked against siblings 2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57
  - evidence: child 59ef AC3 "Assertions check structure/type (keys + JSON primitive types), not exact values (anti-change-detector)" is self-scoped to this story's own tests; 2359 AC4, 33d0 AC4, 822a AC4, 8937 AC4 are likewise self-scoped; no sibling owns a deliverable that PRODUCES a documented guardrail/policy governing FUTURE/new reconciler tests
  - scenarios: a new reconciler test added next cycle has no documented guardrail to conform to; the anti-change-detector intent silently erodes once these stories close
  - impact: parent AC6 cannot be fully checked off by closing the current children as written (4-bucket: PARTIALLY covered — posture applied per-test, documented governing artifact absent)
  - checklist_item: union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion
  - suggested_fix: assign the documented-guardrail deliverable to one story (natural home: 8937, the contract keystone) or add a small dedicated child, so AC6's documented governing artifact has an owner.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: this child FULLY contributes to SC1/AC1 (verified-fake honesty); AC6 PARTIALLY covered at roster level (single absence above); all other parent criteria covered by named siblings; AC7 is structural/aggregate. No coverage finding attributable to child 59ef.
